problems.title: Dog Years
problems.prompt:
Convert a dog's age from human years to dog years.

Background and required method:
The first two human years count as 10.5 dog years each; every additional year counts as 4 dog years. Raise ValueError for a negative age.

Implement this interface:
def dog_years(human_years: float) -> float:

Parameters:
• human_years (float): Non-negative age in human years.

starterFiles.name: main.py

    Args:
        human_years (float): Non-negative age in human years.

    Returns:
        float: Convert a dog's age from human years to dog years.

    Raises:
        ValueError: If human_years is negative.
    """
    pass

problems.title: Sound Level
problems.prompt:
Compare a sound level with familiar reference noises.

Background and required method:
Exact levels are quiet room 40, alarm clock 70, gas lawnmower 106, and jackhammer 130. Between references, describe it as 'between X and Y'; below 40 or above 130 returns 'outside range'.

Parameters:
• decibels (float): Sound level in decibels.

    """
    pass

tests.name: between
tests.code: assert sound_description(80) == 'between alarm clock and gas lawnmower'

problems.title: Musical Note Frequency
problems.prompt:
Calculate the frequency of a named musical note.

Background and required method:
Use C4=261.63 Hz and fourth-octave offsets D4=293.66, E4=329.63, F4=349.23, G4=392.00, A4=440.00, B4=493.88. Multiply by 2^(octave-4).

Implement this interface:
def note_frequency(note: str) -> float:

Parameters:
• note (str): Note name from C0 through B8.

starterFiles.content:

    """Calculate the frequency of a named musical note.

    Args:
        note (str): Note name from C0 through B8.

    Returns:
        float: Calculate the frequency of a named musical note.
    """
    pass

problems.title: Frequency to Note
problems.prompt:
Find the nearest fourth-octave note within a tolerance.

Background and required method:
Compare with these frequencies in hertz: C4=261.63, D4=293.66, E4=329.63, F4=349.23, G4=392.00, A4=440.00, and B4=493.88. Return the note name when its absolute difference is at most tolerance; otherwise return None.

Implement this interface:
def frequency_note(frequency: float, tolerance: float = 1.0) -> str | None:

Parameters:
• frequency (float): Frequency in hertz.
• tolerance (float): Maximum accepted difference in hertz.
problems.signature: def frequency_note(frequency: float, tolerance: float = 1.0) -> str | None:

starterFiles.name: main.py
starterFiles.content:

    Args:

        tolerance (float): Maximum accepted difference in hertz.

    Returns:
        str | None: Find the nearest fourth-octave note within a tolerance.
    """

problems.prompt:

problems.prompt:

problems.title: Cell Phone Bill
problems.prompt:
Calculate and itemize a monthly mobile-phone bill.

Background and required method:
The base charge is $15 for 50 minutes and 50 texts. Extra minutes cost $0.25 and extra texts cost $0.15. Add a $0.44 emergency fee, then apply 5 percent tax to the entire pre-tax bill. Return base, extra_minutes, extra_texts, emergency_fee, tax, and total, each rounded to cents.

Implement this interface:

problems.title: Roulette Payout Simulation
problems.prompt:
Simulate one American roulette spin and list every winning bet category.

Background and required method:
Use a local random.Random(seed) to select one of 38 equally likely pockets: 0, 00, and 1 through 36. The payout list always includes the selected pocket. For 1-36 also include red/black, odd/even, and 1-18/19-36. Red numbers are 1,3,5,7,9,12,14,16,18,19,21,23,25,27,30,32,34,36; the remaining numbered pockets are black. Zero pockets have no outside payouts.

Implement this interface:
def roulette_spin(seed: int | None = None) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:

Parameters:
• seed (int | None): Optional seed for a reproducible spin.
problems.signature: def roulette_spin(seed: int | None = None) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:

starterFiles.name: main.py
starterFiles.content:
import random

def roulette_spin(seed: int | None = None) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:
    """Simulate one American roulette spin and list every winning bet category.

    Args:
        seed (int | None): Optional seed for a reproducible spin.

    Returns:
        tuple[str, list[str]]: Simulate one American roulette spin and list every winning bet category.
    """
    pass

tests.name: valid pocket
tests.code: assert roulette_spin(2)[0] in ['0','00']+[str(n) for n in range(1,37)] == True

tests.name: straight bet included
tests.code: assert (lambda r: r[0] in r[1])(roulette_spin(8)) == True

tests.name: zero rules
tests.code: assert all(len(bets)==1 for seed in range(100) for pocket,bets in [roulette_spin(seed)] if pocket in ('0','00')) == True
